list.name: trace_id
list.label: Trace ID (exemplar)
list.description: Set by clicking an exemplar diamond (or paste a trace_id). Filters the 'Exemplar → Request Log' panel.

panels.title: Default NGINX (stub_status) — everything vanilla nginx exposes
Requests / s
panels.description: nginx stub_status total request rate — the only request signal vanilla nginx exposes.

panels.title: Avg Latency
panels.description: Avg request duration (µs metric → ms). Per-route/upstream breakdowns below — dimensions stub_status cannot produce.

panels.title: Request Duration Distribution
panels.description: Exp-histogram latency distribution (µs). The shape — not just an average — is the OTel value-add.

panels.title: Request Latency by Status (avg µs) · ⬦ exemplars → click to Loki
panels.description: Avg request duration (µs) per status class, with EXEMPLAR diamonds: each ⬦ is one sampled real request placed at its own latency (its native-histogram bucket), tagged with the W3C trace_id. Click a diamond → "View tail log (Loki)" to open the matching access tail LogRecord (joined by trace_id). This is the metric→exemplar→log drill-down.

panels.title: Slowest Routes (avg µs · http.route)
panels.description: Per-route latency (location name) — only possible because the module emits a per-route histogram.

panels.title: Avg Duration by Upstream Zone (µs · nginx.upstream.zone)
Traces (nginx server spans via Tempo)
nginx Server Spans (Tempo)
panels.description: nginx server spans emitted by ngx-otel-rust. Enable the `traces` compose profile to start receiving spans. Click an exemplar diamond on the 'Request Latency' panel to jump directly to the matching span here.
nginx OTel server spans (root traces) via Tempo TraceQL. Click a trace to open it; from a span use 'Logs for this span' to reach the matching Loki access-tail log.

panels.title: Exemplar → Request Log  (trace_id = $trace_id)
panels.description: The access tail LogRecord for the exemplar(s) selected above. Click an exemplar diamond on the latency panel to set $trace_id, or paste one into the Trace ID box. trace_id is the join key between the duration-histogram exemplar and this log line.

panels.title: Error Log (nginx.error — coalesced)
panels.description: OTel error-log signal: coalesced nginx error LogRecords (severity ≥ warn). Body = verbatim message; nginx.error.coalesced_count = flood size.

panels.title: Serving Certificates — Time to Expiration
panels.description: TLS serving certificates as enumerated from the live SSL_CTX at startup/reload (ngx_otel.tls.certificate.time_to_expiration). What nginx SERVES, not what is on disk: a renewed file changes nothing until reload. Negative = expired. Thresholds: red < 7d, orange < 30d.
